summary: 23:30Z cycle. Sprint progress: #1209 closed — PR #1243 merged (MCP prefix match fix in AAB). One kernel win this cycle. PR #1238 (fix #1208 heredoc) still BEHIND main — rebase blocker persists 2nd consecutive EM run → director escalation triggered per policy. Senior still has no branch on #1202 (KE-2 flagship) after 3 cycles — flagged. #1139 unstarted. Test suite 4394/4394 passing.

Rebase will not encounter conflicts (no overlap) but copilot-cli must act. This is run 2 of needs-rebase — escalating per policy.

mergedThisCycle.note: Merged between 20:30Z and 23:30Z. MCP server name prefix match fix: aab.ts now uses startsWith() rather than exact match for mcp.call target normalization. Closes #1209.

prBudget.note: Kernel squad: 1 PR open (#1238) — within budget. Repo total 4: #1238 (kernel), #1244 (HQ EM report), #1246 (remove Rust kernel), #1247 (site fix). Only kernel-owned PR counts against squad budget.

openSprintIssues.title: AAB normalization failure — 3,825+ unintended default-deny decisions (KE-2 ActionContext)
openSprintIssues.technicalNote: Now the #1 sprint priority. Strip rtk/time/timeout prefixes in aab.ts before pattern matching. Emit UnknownActionType WARNING event. Audit tool-action-map.json. Evidence: ~3,825 unintended denials from 5.5-day database slice. See dogfood issues #1205, #1203, #1197, #1196, #1195 for additional test case evidence.

notableIssues.title: Swarm Health Alert — 2026-03-28
notableIssues.label: priority:P0
notableIssues.note: 85.7% stuck worker rate (12/14 agents). No change. Required ops actions: git worktree prune, clean /tmp/ PID files, fix cron collisions (0 6 * * * and 45 */6 * * *), verify cron daemon, address 4x oversubscription (133 agents vs 32 max_workers). Kernel squad cannot resolve — ops/director action required.

blockers.description: PR #1238 branch BEHIND main for 2 consecutive EM runs. copilot-cli agent has not rebased. This now satisfies escalation rule: blocker persists across 2 runs.
blockers.action: copilot-cli must run: git rebase origin/main on the PR #1238 branch, force-push, confirm CI green

escalations.description: PR #1238 rebase blocker persists across 2 consecutive EM runs (20:30Z and 23:30Z). Policy: blocker persisting 2+ runs → escalate to director. copilot-cli agent has not actioned the rebase since the 20:30Z run. Manual intervention or director redirect may be needed.
recommendedActions: Director to ping copilot-cli agent to rebase PR #1238 on main HEAD
Alternatively, a human can rebase the branch directly: git checkout <copilot-branch> && git rebase origin/main && git push --force-with-lease

recommendedActions: Clean dead PID files in /tmp/ (copilot-detached-*.pid etc.)
Address 4x oversubscription: 133 enabled agents vs 32 max_workers

escalationRules.notes: persistentBlocker triggered: PR #1238 rebase unresolved across 2 runs — escalated to director. #1186 P0 swarm health cycle 6 — ops escalation repeated. Senior agent unstarted on #1202 for 3 cycles.

dogfoodObservations.description: Every rtk git * and rtk gh * command in this EM run is subject to AAB normalization failure until #1202 is fixed. Commands prefixed with rtk fall through to unknown action type. Confirms live impact on EM and all agent workflows. ~3,825 unintended denials over 5.5 days remains an accurate lower bound — likely higher with swarm growth.

PR #1238 BEHIND main but no automated rebase signal was sent to copilot-cli. The governance harness knows a branch is behind (via mergeStateStatus) but has no mechanism to trigger a rebase task for the owning agent. This is a swarm coordination gap — the director or EM must manually escalate.
dogfoodObservations.suggestedFix: Add an automated rebase-reminder event: when mergeStateStatus=BEHIND for >1 EM cycle, emit a directive to the PR-owning agent. This aligns with the FileScopeViolation and HeartbeatMissed patterns already in the event schema.

dogfoodObservations.description: Multiple dogfood denial issues (#1205, #1203, #1197, #1196, #1195) are all traceable to the AAB unknown-action-type fallthrough from #1202. These are not separate bugs — they are evidence of the same normalization gap. Closing #1202 should cascade-close most of these.
